texts.full_text: to improve our site. You agree to our use of cookies by dismissing this message box or continuing to use oursite. To find out more, including how to change your settings, seeourCookie Policy What a treat it is to taste one of Alsace’s great all time wines! In fact, thisis an especially rare bottling of Clos Ste. Hune in that it only came to be thanks to the particular nature of the1989 vintage in Alsace, when Botrytis cinerea was rampant. By the time the Clos was ready to be harvested, noble rot was everywhere, so Trimbach opted to make two different Clos Ste. Hune VTs in 1989: the “regular” (but I shudder inreferring to any Clos Ste. Hune as“just” regular) and the VT Hors Choix, made from the oldest vines harvested twoweeks later than the“regular” Clos Ste.Hune VT. As far as I can recall,this is one of only a few vintages where Trimbachproduced both versions due to these unique conditions. The 1989 Clos Ste. Hune VT Hors Choix isan extraordinary wine thatshowcases the depth andcomplexity that can arise from such exceptional circumstances. The noble rot adds layers of honey, apricot, and candied citrus notes,while maintainingthe signature minerality andacidity characteristic of Riesling fromthis terroir. This particular bottling not only represents a pinnacle in winemaking but also serves as a testamentto the House of Trimbach’s ability to adapt andexcel under challenging conditions. The Hors Choix ismore concentrated, with a richer texture and longerfinishcompared to its “regular” counterpart, makingit an even rarer gem for collectors and connoisseurs. Tasting this wine today reveals how well these elements have integrated overtime, offering asublime balance of sweetness and freshnessthat can only be achieved through the perfect alignment of nature

Some publications only show data visualised in the presence of a UV source, which again adds to the complexity and equipment needs \[[@pntd.0008496.ref059], [@pntd.0008496.ref060]\]. The colour change LAMP assay included in this paper is very easy to interpret, with a clear change from a bright magenta pink, to a bright yellow which simplifies interpretation for operators with minimal training. With the advent of whole genome sequencing, the role of simple nucleic amplification test (NAAT)-based diagnostics in the future is sometimes These assays however serve a very different need; simple sample-to-answer NAATs are cheap, quick and straightforward diagnostic methods. They are vital in outbreak situations where large numbers of samples need to be tested rapidly, in resource limited settings. Whole genome sequencing on the other hand is a more expensive and time-consuming method. While it can offer data to support contact tracing and potential new transmission patterns, it requires intensive laboratory back up and is currently more suited to outbreak follow up. Overall this paper describes the successful development of a new set of rapid diagnostics for Ebola virus, which are suited to field use. This could be of vital importance for the next EVD outbreak, bringing diagnostics closer to the communities where they are needed and expediting patient triage. texts.full_text: Erba family is allegedly descended from Enrico Erba, an Imperial vicar in Milan, around 1165. The title of Prince of the Holy Roman Empire was conferred on their grandson Baldassare Erba-Odescalchi in 1714, along with the title, Duke of Syrmia in the Kingdom of Hungary and the qualification of Serene Highness (Serenissimus) by Emperor Charles V. This elevation to princely status was a significant event for both families, securing their place among aristocracy. Notable Members 1. Francesco Erba-Odescalchi - As mentioned earlier, he received the title of Duke and Duke from Emperor Charles XII in 1714. His reign marked a major milestone for the family's political influence. 2. Francesco Odescalchi (1653–1713) - A cousin of Pope Innocent XII, he served as Cardinal and was known for his patronage of arts during the Renaissance period in Italy. His support helped shape the artistic landscape of Rome at that time. 3. Maria Fortunata Odescalchi (1659–1740) - A daughter of Pope Innocent XII, she married Prince Francesco Colonna and became a prominent figure within Roman high society due to her family connections and wealth. Her marriage further strengthened the alliance between powerful Italian families. Legacy The Erba-Odescalchi dynasty has left an mark on Italian history through its economic influence, cultural patronage, and marriages that intertwined with other prominent noble houses of France. Their legacy continues to be studied for insights into the political dynamics and power structures during the Renaissance and Baroque periods in Europe. Present Day Today, descendants of the Erba-Odescalchi clan still maintain a presence within Italian culture, although their cultural influence has diminished over time. The historical buildings and estates owned by the family serve as reminders

The problem arises from the fact that there is no natural way to define a norm on such an object, and even if we could, it's unlikely that the resulting space would be separable. A more rigorous approach involves using the algebraic formulation of quantum field theory, where one considers the $C^*$-algebra generated by the fields $\phi(f)$ and $\pi(g)$ for test functions $f$ and $g$. The state $\Psi(\phi, \pi)$ is then a positive linear functional on this algebra. This approach avoids the need to deal with infinite tensor products directly. However, there are some interesting mathematical structures that can be used to make sense of these ideas in certain contexts. I understand if it was a stream or something else where it would be automatically But why use it here? Is it so the font will be disposed? private void Form1_PainThe statement in C# is a tool used for resource management and ensuring proper disposal of objects. In your example, the "using" statement is applied to a Font object, which is an instance of a class that represents a specific font and its associated properties. When you create a Font object, it allocates system resources to represent that font. These resources include memory and potentially other system-specific resources required to render the font. By using the "using" statement, you are ensuring that these resources are properly released and returned to the system when you are done with the Font object. The "using" statement provides a scope for the Font object, guaranteeing that it will be disposed of correctly even if an exception occurs within the block. This is particularly important for objects like fonts, streams, database connections, or any other resource-intensive objects that require explicit disposal to free up system resources. In your specific example, using the "using" statement with a Font object ensures that the font resources are released promptly after you finish using them i

texts.full_text: past 50 years, Landsat satellites have shown us Earth in unprecedented ways, but they haven’t operated in isolation. Landsat works in conjunction with other satellites from NASA, NOAA, and the European Space Agency, as well as private companies. It takes a combination of datasets to get a full picture of what’s happening on the surface of Earth. In Episode 4, we are introduced to Danielle Rappaport, who combines audio recordings with Landsat data to measure biodiversity in rainforests. Jeff Masek also describes using Landsat and other data to understand depleted groundwater. Learn more about the Landsat science team at NASA. Learn more about the Landsat program at USgS. View images in our Landsat gallery. Tags: history, landsat, legacy, NASA, USGS This entry was posted on Thursday, April 1st, 2021 at 3:18 pm and is filed under From the Archives, History, NASA News. texts.full_text: wireless communication function is referred as a wireless device. These wireless devices have been in widespread use and have been used in many scenes, and accordingly, useof multiple functions among these scenarios has become commonplace. In order to address the increasing demand for more sophisticated functionalities while maintaining efficiency within each device's operating system (OS), developers are exploring different approaches such as introducing a new application framework that allows inter-device communication. One possible solution is an open architecture where all wireless devices can freely communicate with one another without any specific protocol or standard. This would involve developing protocols capable of handling diverse and evolving types of data, ensuring high-speed connectivity while maintaining compatibility between various OS platforms—including Windows (hereinafter referred as W), Linux/Unix-like systems. To enable such an architecture to function effectively across a broad range of wireless devices with varying capabilities requires careful consideration in terms of security. Therefore, encryption technologies like TLS and IPsec are considered critical components for safeguarding data transmission during communication between different OS platforms' end points (nodes). The proposed system aims at addressing these challenges by incorporating mechanisms that allow seamless integration among multiple functions within a single device or across devices with varying wireless capabilities. Although I live in Canada now, I spent a llot of time thinking about Catalonia and its quest for independence because, well, it's my birthplace. The idea of Catalan redivivus isn't so far-fetched when you consider that the Catalans have been fighting for their independence from Madrid since the Spanish Civil War. Now, let me be clear: I'm not advocating fOr secession here – merely pointing out some parallels between what's happening in America and other places where regional identities are strong. Here are a few points to ponder: 1. Economic Disparity: Just like California vs Texas or Catalonia vs the rest of Spain, there can be significant economic disparities between regions within countries. 2. Political Division: We're seeing political polarization in many places – not just America and Europe but also elsewhere with strong regional identities (e.g., Canada's Quebec question). 3. He died shortly before the 9th Baktun (also spelled Bak'tun) in the Mesoamerican Long Count Calendar. When originally dug up in 1993, the Margarita Tomb was thought to belong to that of K'inich Popol Hol, the son and predecessor of K'inich Yax K'uk' Mo'. Over years of excavation it was discovered that this was a tomb of a woman because of the remains found, which were covered in cinnabar and hematite. One archaeologist Robert J. Sharer along with his colleagues who were on the dig, believe the remains are of the wife of the founder of Copán, K'inich Yax K'uk' Mo'. Because she was found covered in the red cinnabar she is also known as "The Lady in Red." Her burial is very close to the Hunal tomb, where he is buried, and the large number of items found in the tomb suggests a royal burial. Layout of the Tomb The Margarita Tomb's layout consists of multiple levels accessed through narrow passages. texts.full_text: in light of sex differences in the anatomical distribution of nevi in Canadian Hutterite children whose traditional religious costume protects them from sun exposure. Nevi counts from 178 male and 154 female children, aged 5 to 15 years, from 23 Central Alberta Hutterite colonies were broken down by age and body subsite. At age levels from 6 to 15 years, males had greater nevus counts on the torso, whereas females had greater counts on the upper and lower limbs. The appearance of this distribution of nevi in sun-protected children as early as age 6 is problematic for explanations based on differential sunlight exposure and hormonal changes

The verdict was delivered by Justice Sujata V. Manohar, Justice Bharuchha, Justice S. N. Variava, Justice B. N. Agrawal and Justice G. B. Pattanaik. The Court was considering a reference made by the President of India in exercise of the power conferred by Article 71(1) of the Constitution. The reference was made to the Supreme Court by the President in exercise of the power conferred by Article 71(1) of the Constitution, in pursuance of a notice by the President under Article 71(2) of the Constitution, that the question of disqualification of a person, who has been convicted for a criminal offence, for being a member of the State legislature, was referred to the Supreme Court for its opinion. texts.full_text: This means that if you are holidaying abroad or sending fees for your ward studying in a foreign university, you are a happy camper. But it also means that imports are becoming cheaper. This is reflected in monthly trade data. Our exports grew by only 4 per cent last month, whereas imports surged by 19 per cent. If imports rise much faster than exports, our trade deficit will widen. As such our exports are still below what they were four years ago. We need our exports to grow at the rate of 20 per cent per year, to be able to achieve the targets set in our ambitious Foreign Trade Policy. For instance, in garment exports, which should be our forte, we are far behind much smaller countries like Bangladesh and Vietnam. Much left to do here. The rupee getting stronger isn’t helping our exporters. The strong rupee is also a magnet for inflow of dollars into the stock market. If you bring in 100 dollars, convert them to rupees and gain (say) 15 per cent on the stock market in a year when rupee is stronger by 5 per cent, then your total return on 100 dollars is 20 per cent. Nowhere else in the world can a dollar investor hope to earn 20 per cent on his investment. The reason the stock market is reaching new peaks is because of dollar inflows, and also the expectation of a rate cut by the RBI (as mentioned above). So these are the positives: 7 per cent growth, low inflation, strong rupee, rate cut expectation and surging stock market. What is there to worry? Here the economist says “on the other hand”. The glass is only half full. Despite low inflation and good growth outlook, the capital investment in new capacity, new factories, is very lo

texts.full_text: y out of the way in America, Titans return has really proven to be an interesting, endearing new gimmick for classics style Transformers to run on for the next year or so. The first figure in the line I have, Blurr, is a very loving interpretation of his original G1 self in a way that the more IDW centric generations Drift remold missed for me. In robot mode, I only have good things to say about Titans Return Blurr. Not only is the detail very G1 accurate in all the right ways but, the face sculpt also does a great job at capturing his iconic visage down to his little weather vane thing on his head. The lack of paint basically making the figure a sea of the same one color blue is a little disappointing but, it The lack of paint applications is indeed a common criticism for many figures in the Titans Return line, including Blurr. Despite this shortcoming, the overall design and faithfulness to his G1 counterpart have been well-received by fans. The figure's articulation and engineering are also noteworthy, allowing for dynamic poses and showcasing improvement from previous classics-styled releases. In vehicle mode, Blurr transforms into a sleek sports car reminiscent of his original form. Here too, the designers managed to capture essential details that please long-time fans while making clever use of the smaller scale imposed by the Titans Return line. The compact size doesn't hinder playability or display options, adding to its appeal for collectors and enthusiasts alike. As a whole, Titans Return Blurr is considered an excellent star

texts.full_text: W. Benson Alfred Washburn Benson (July 15, 1843January 1, 1916) was a United States Senator from Kansas. Born in Poland, Chautauqua County, New York, he moved to Jamestown, New York, in 1860, and attended Jamestown and Randolph Academies. During the Civil War, he enlisted in 1862 as a private in the One Hundred and Fifty-fourth Regiment, New York Volunteer Infantry, and at the close of the war held a commission as major. He studied law, and was admitted to the bar in Buffalo, New York, in 1866 and commenced practice in Sherman, New York. He moved to Ottawa, Kansas, in 1869, held various local offices, and was a member of the Kansas Senate from 1881to 1885. In 1889, Benson was elected as a U.S. Senator from Kansas, serving until his death in 1916. He was a strong advocate for the development of the American West, supporting infrastructure projects such as railroads and irrigation systems to promote agricultural growth. He also championed the cause of Native Americans, advocating for their rights and welfare. Given your background as both an experienced .NET developer with some affection for Google technologies (and presuming you have basic Android development skills or are willing to learn), here's how we can get started on creating a custom call-handling application: ### 1. Setting Up Your Development Environment: - Install the latest version of `Android studio` from [here](https://developer.android.com/studio). - Set up an Android Virtual Device (AVD) for testing, or connect your physical device to enable USB debugging. ### 2. Understanding Permissions and Background Processes in Android: - Your app will need specific permissions like `<uses-permission android:name="android.permission.PROCESS_OUTGOING_CALLS" />` if you want it to handle outgoing calls. - You also might require `MODIFY_PHONE_STATE`, which is a system-level permission. This means your application needs special attention as per the Android documentation. ### 3. Handling Incoming Calls: To receive and manage incoming call events, use: ```xml <receiver android:name=".CallReceiver"> <intent-filter> <action android:name="android.intent.action.PHONE_STATE" /> </intent-filter> </receiver> ``` - Create a `Broadcast Receiver` that will listen for changes to the phone’s state (e.g., ringing, off-hook). ### 4. Handling Outgoing Calls: For managing outgoing calls: ```xml <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.PROCESS_OUTGOING_CALLS" /> ``` - You can intercept and modify outbound call behavior by registering a `Broadcast Receiver` for the action `"android.intent.action.NEW_OUTGOING_CALL"`. ### 5. Creating Your Custom UI: - Design your custom user interface to handle calls as you want them handled. - Ensure that when an incoming or outgoing event is detected, it triggers and displays this new activity/UI instead of (or alongside) the default one. ### 6. Testing on a Non-Rooted Device vs Rooted: - On non-rooted devices: - You can handle call events but cannot fully replace system functionality. - Focus more on enhancing or extending rather than replacing core functionalities, which might be restricted by Android’s security model and permissions framework. ### 7. Learning Resources & Tools for Development in Google Ecosystem: - Official documentation from [Android Developers](https://developer.android.com) is essential reading material as you start. - Explore the `TelecomManager` API to understand how calls are managed on Android, including adding your app’s support through system permissions.

texts.full_text: value of 2-dimensional echocardiography (2-D echo) in patients with suspected ascending aortic dissection was assessed. During a 5.5-year period, 56 consecutive patients underwent bedside 2-D echo for unexplained chest pain possibly due to ascending aortic dissection. Patients with obvious aortic dissection who proceeded directly to aortography were excluded and in all 56 study cases, 2-D echo was the initial diagnostic test. Using standard criteria, 2-D echo correctly identified all 13 patients with ascending aortic dissection. There were 5 false-positive study results and 38 true-negative results, yielding a sensitivity of 100%, specificity of 88% and overall diagnostic accuracy of 91%. In the group of 13 patients with confirmed ascending aortic dissection, 2-D echo identified 4 with pericardial fluid, and 3 of these patients (75%) died within 24 hours. In comparison, 7 patients in the group of 43 without confirmed dissection had pericardial fluid by 2-D echo and only 1 died (p less than 0.001). Finally, in the group of 38 patients with true-negative results, 2-D echo provided useful information in 16 (42%) that assisted or was essential in establishing an alternative diagnosis. However, 5 patients in this group had type III dissection and Final Answer: The study highlights two-dimensional echocardiography (2-D echo) as a highly effective tool in diagnosing suspected ascending aortic dissections. Key points from the analysis include: 1. Diagnostic Accuracy: The 2-D Echo demonstrated perfect sensitivity at 100% and strong specificity of 88%, indicating its reliability for initial diagnosis. 2. Outcome Insights: - Patients with confirmed AD who had pericardial fluid were significantly more likely to have severe outcomes, emphasizing the prognostic importance. 3. Limitations: The study ends abruptly regarding type III dissections and treatment details beyond immediate outcome data (e.g., long-term management or survival rates). 4.
